keynotes.text: A small, herbaceous plant, with branched stems and pink or yellow flowers, widely used in Chile as a mild tonic.

indications.text: Stimulant bitter, tonic. Useful in dyspepsia and indigestion. An infusion may be made of 1 oz. to 1 pint of boiling water.

dosing.text: Of infusion - a wineglassful. Of fluid extract - 1/2 to 1 drachm.

generalities.text: Erythraea acaulis , a native of Southern Algeria, has roots that yield a yellow dye. Sabatia angularis , or American Centaury, is a simple bitter used as a tonic and antiperiodic, in doses of 1 drachm of fluid extract or decoction of the whole plant. It has been found to contain a small proportion of Erythrocentaurin. The root of S. Elliottii is used in a similar manner in the south-eastern United States, and the whole plant of S. campestris in the south-western. S. Elliottii is known as the Quinine Flower, its properties resembling quinine. Purchase from Richters Similar: Centaury (Centaurium erythraea) Seeds and Dried Herb
